_meta.sourceOfRecord: 26 CFR 1.224-1, Table 1 to paragraph (h), fetched from eCFR (codified text governs; FR preamble used for rulemaking history only)
_meta.notes: Spec 8 open uncertainty 2: a pre-launch re-check should diff the eCFR table row-by-row against the govinfo.gov FR PDF before major reuse. Category counts verified: 10+11+4+9+10+11+6+10 = 71.

finalRule.applicability: taxable years beginning after December 31, 2024 (deduction runs 2025-2028 per IRC 224(h))

rejectedOccupations.label: Retail cashiers
rejectedOccupations.explanation: Retail cashiers are not a listed occupation. In the final rule the Treasury Department and IRS found that self-identified cashiers who received tips were actually working in roles already covered by Fast Food and Counter Workers (code 107) or Hotel, Motel, and Resort Desk Clerks (code 303), so no separate cashier category was added.

rejectedOccupations.label: Accountants
rejectedOccupations.explanation: Accountants were considered and not included in the final list — the Treasury Department and IRS found no supporting tip-income data for the occupation. Tips received in a non-listed occupation are still taxable as usual, but they do not qualify for the federal tips deduction.

rejectedOccupations.label: Tax preparers
rejectedOccupations.explanation: Tax preparers were considered and not included in the final list for lack of supporting tip-income data. Tips received in a non-listed occupation do not qualify for the federal tips deduction.

rejectedOccupations.label: Chiropractors
rejectedOccupations.explanation: Chiropractors were considered and not included in the final list for lack of supporting tip-income data. Tips received in a non-listed occupation do not qualify for the federal tips deduction.

rejectedOccupations.label: Concert merchandise sellers
rejectedOccupations.explanation: Concert merchandise sellers were considered and not included in the final list for lack of supporting tip-income data. Tips received in a non-listed occupation do not qualify for the federal tips deduction.

occupations.title: Bartenders
occupations.description: Mix and serve drinks or other refreshments to patrons, directly or through waitstaff
occupations.examples: Barkeep, mixologist, taproom attendant, sommelier

occupations.title: Wait Staff
occupations.description: Take orders and serve food and beverages to patrons at tables in dining establishments or at catered events
occupations.examples: Cocktail waitress, dining car server, banquet staff

occupations.title: Food or Beverage Servers, Non-restaurant
occupations.description: Serve food or beverages to individuals outside of a restaurant environment, such as in hotel rooms, residential care facilities, or cars
occupations.examples: Room service food server, boat hop, beer cart server

occupations.title: Dining Room and Cafeteria Attendants and Bartender Helpers
occupations.description: Facilitate food service. Clean tables; remove dirty dishes; replace soiled table linens; set tables; replenish supply of clean linens, silverware, glassware, and dishes; supply service bar with food; and serve items such as water, condiments, and coffee to patrons
occupations.examples: Bar back, bar helper, busser

occupations.title: Chefs and Cooks
occupations.description: Direct and may participate in the preparation, seasoning, and cooking of salads, soups, fish, meats, vegetables, desserts, or other foods
occupations.examples: Executive chef, pastry chef, sous chef, fast food cook, private chef, restaurant cook, saucier, food truck cook, banquet cook, caterer, chocolatier, confectioner

occupations.title: Food Preparation Workers
occupations.description: Perform a variety of food preparation duties other than cooking, such as preparing cold foods and shellfish, slicing meat, and brewing coffee or tea
occupations.examples: Salad maker, sandwich maker, fruit and vegetable parer, kitchen steward

occupations.title: Fast Food and Counter Workers
occupations.description: Serve customers at counter or from a steam table. Perform duties such as taking orders and serving food and beverages. May take payment. May prepare food and beverages
occupations.examples: Barista, ice cream server, cafeteria server

occupations.title: Dishwashers
occupations.description: Clean dishes, kitchen, food preparation equipment, or utensils
occupations.examples: Dish room worker, silverware cleaner

occupations.title: Host Staff, Restaurant, Lounge, and Coffee Shop
occupations.description: Welcome patrons, seat them at tables or in lounge, and help ensure quality of facilities and service
occupations.examples: Maître d'hôtel, dining room host

occupations.title: Bakers
occupations.description: Mix and bake ingredients to produce breads, rolls, cookies, cakes, pies, pastries, or other baked goods
occupations.examples: Bread baker, cake baker, bagel baker, pastry finisher

occupations.title: Gambling Dealers
occupations.description: Operate gambling games. Stand or sit behind table and operate games of chance by dispensing the appropriate number of cards or blocks to players or operating other gambling equipment. Distribute winnings or collect players' money or chips. May compare the house's hand against players' hands
occupations.examples: Blackjack dealer, craps dealer, poker dealer, roulette dealer, pit clerk

occupations.title: Gambling Change Persons and Booth Cashiers
occupations.description: Exchange coins, tokens, and chips for patrons' money. May issue payoffs and obtain customer's signature on receipt. May operate a booth in the slot machine area and furnish change persons with money bank at the start of the shift, or count and audit money in drawers
occupations.examples: Slot attendant, mutuel teller

occupations.title: Gambling Cage Workers
occupations.description: In a gambling establishment, conduct financial transactions for patrons. Accept patron's credit application and verify credit references to provide check-cashing authorization or to establish house credit accounts. May reconcile daily summaries of transactions to balance books. May sell gambling chips, tokens, or tickets to patrons, or to other workers for resale to patrons. May convert gambling chips, tokens, or tickets to currency upon patron's request. May use a cash register or computer to record transaction
occupations.examples: Casino cashier, cage cashier

occupations.title: Gambling and Sports Book Writers and Runners
occupations.description: Post information enabling patrons to wager on various races and sporting events. Assist in the operation of games such as keno and bingo. May operate random number-generating equipment and announce the numbers for patrons. Receive, verify, and record patrons' wagers. Scan and process winning tickets presented by patrons and pay out winnings for those wagers
occupations.examples: Betting runner, bingo worker, keno runner, race book writer

occupations.title: Dancers
occupations.description: Perform dances
occupations.examples: Club dancer, dance artist

occupations.title: Musicians and Singers
occupations.description: Play one or more musical instruments or sing
occupations.examples: Instrumentalist, accompanist, lounge singer

occupations.title: Disc Jockeys, Except Radio
occupations.description: Play prerecorded music for live audiences at venues or events such as clubs, parties, or wedding receptions. May use techniques such as mixing, cutting, or sampling to manipulate recordings. May also perform as emcee (master of ceremonies)
occupations.examples: Deejay, club DJ

occupations.title: Entertainers and Performers
occupations.description: Entertain audiences with artistic expression
occupations.examples: Comedian, clown, magician, street performer

occupations.title: Digital Content Creators
occupations.description: Produce and publish on digital platforms original entertainment and personality-driven content, such as live streams, short-form videos, or podcasts
occupations.examples: Streamer, online video creator, social media influencer, podcaster

occupations.title: Ushers, Lobby Attendants, and Ticket Takers
occupations.description: Assist patrons at entertainment events by performing duties, such as collecting admission tickets and passes from patrons, assisting in finding seats, searching for lost articles, and helping patrons locate such facilities as restrooms and telephones
occupations.examples: Ticket collector, theater usher

occupations.title: Locker Room, Coatroom, and Dressing Room Attendants
occupations.description: Provide personal items to patrons or customers in locker rooms, dressing rooms, or coatrooms
occupations.examples: Coat checker, washroom attendant, bathhouse attendant

occupations.title: Baggage Porters and Bellhops
occupations.description: Handle baggage for travelers at transportation terminals or for guests at hotels or similar establishments
occupations.examples: Hotel baggage handler, curbside airport check-in assistant, doorman

occupations.title: Concierges
occupations.description: Assist patrons at hotels or apartment buildings with personal services. May take messages; arrange or give advice on transportation, business services, or entertainment; or monitor guest requests for housekeeping and maintenance
occupations.examples: Hotel guest service agent, activities concierge

occupations.title: Hotel, Motel, and Resort Desk Clerks
occupations.description: Accommodate hotel, motel, and resort patrons by registering and assigning rooms to guests, issuing room keys or cards, transmitting and receiving messages, keeping records of occupied rooms and guests' accounts, making and confirming reservations, and presenting statements to and collecting payments from departing guests
occupations.examples: Front desk clerk, registration clerk

occupations.title: Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
occupations.description: Perform any combination of light cleaning duties to maintain commercial establishments, such as hotels, in a clean and orderly manner. Duties may include making beds, replenishing linens, cleaning rooms and halls, and vacuuming
occupations.examples: Hotel maid, housekeeping staff

occupations.title: Home Maintenance and Repair Workers
occupations.description: Perform work to keep machines, mechanical equipment, or the structure of a building in repair. May maintain and repair musical instruments, furniture, antiques, and non-fixtures
occupations.examples: Handyman, roofer, window repairer, house painter (interior or exterior), flooring installer, piano tuner, furniture restorer, antique repairer

occupations.title: Home Landscaping and Groundskeeping Workers
occupations.description: Landscape or maintain grounds of property using hand or power tools or equipment. Workers typically perform a variety of tasks, which may include any combination of the following: sod laying, mowing, trimming, planting, watering, fertilizing, digging, raking, sprinkler installation, and installation of mortarless segmental concrete masonry wall units
occupations.examples: Lawn mower, gardener, tree trimmer, weed sprayer

occupations.title: Home Electricians
occupations.description: Install, maintain, and repair electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures. Ensure that work is in accordance with relevant codes. May install or service exterior lights, intercom systems, or electrical control systems
occupations.examples: Electrician

occupations.title: Home Plumbers
occupations.description: Assemble, install, alter, and repair pipelines or pipe systems that carry water, steam, air, or other liquids or gases. May install heating and cooling equipment and mechanical control systems
occupations.examples: Plumber, pipefitter, steamfitter, sprinkler installer

occupations.title: Home Heating and Air Conditioning Mechanics and Installers
occupations.description: Install or repair heating, central air conditioning, HVAC, or refrigeration systems, including oil burners, hot-air furnaces, and heating stoves
occupations.examples: Air conditioning repairer, heating system installer, chimney sweep

occupations.title: Home Appliance Installers and Repairers
occupations.description: Repair, adjust, or install all types of electric or gas household appliances, such as refrigerators, washers, dryers, and ovens
occupations.examples: Washing machine installer, dishwasher repairer

occupations.title: Home Cleaning Service Workers
occupations.description: Perform any combination of light cleaning duties to maintain private households in a clean and orderly manner. Duties may include making beds, replenishing linens, cleaning rooms and halls, and vacuuming
occupations.examples: House cleaner, pool cleaner, carpet cleaner, window washer

occupations.title: Locksmiths
occupations.description: Repair and open locks, make keys, change locks and safe combinations, and install and repair safes
occupations.examples: Safe installer, key maker

occupations.title: Roadside Assistance Workers
occupations.description: Provide on-road assistance to drivers whose vehicles have broken down
occupations.examples: Tow truck driver, car battery technician, tire repairer, tire changer, car fuel deliverer

occupations.title: Personal Care and Service Workers
occupations.description: Provide personalized assistance to individuals with disabilities or illness who require help with personal care and activities of daily living support (for example, feeding, bathing, dressing, grooming, toileting, and ambulation). May also provide help with tasks such as preparing meals, doing light housekeeping, and doing laundry. Work is performed in various settings depending on the needs of the care recipient and may include locations such as their home, place of work, out in the community, at a daytime nonresidential facility, or a residential facility
occupations.examples: Elderly companion, personal care aide, butler, house sitter, personal valet

occupations.title: Private Event Planners
occupations.description: Coordinate activities of staff or clients to make arrangements for private events. May provide creative design for décor and invitations
occupations.examples: Wedding planner, party planner

occupations.title: Private Event and Portrait Photographers
occupations.description: Photograph people, landscapes, or other subjects. May use lighting equipment to enhance a subject's appearance. May use editing software to produce finished images and prints
occupations.examples: Wedding photographer, headshot photographer

occupations.title: Private Event Videographers
occupations.description: Operate video or film camera to record images or scenes of private events
occupations.examples: Wedding videographer

occupations.title: Event Officiants
occupations.description: Lead and facilitate the ceremony for life events such as weddings or funerals. Ceremonies may be religious or civil services
occupations.examples: Wedding officiant, funeral celebrant, clergy, vow renewal officiant

occupations.title: Pet and Show Animal Caretakers
occupations.description: Feed, water, groom, bathe, exercise, or otherwise provide care to promote and maintain the well-being of pets or show animals
occupations.examples: Pet groomer, pet sitter, pet walker, kennel worker, pet trainer, horse groomer

occupations.title: Tutors
occupations.description: Instruct individual students or small groups of students in academic subjects to supplement formal class instruction or to prepare students for standardized or admissions tests. May provide instruction in person or remotely
occupations.examples: Reading tutor, math tutor, language tutor

occupations.title: Nannies and Babysitters
occupations.description: Attend to children at businesses and private households. Perform a variety of tasks, such as dressing, feeding, bathing, and overseeing play
occupations.examples: Au pair, child sitter at hotels and gyms

occupations.title: Visual Artists
occupations.description: Create original visual artwork using any of a wide variety of media and techniques
occupations.examples: Ice sculptor, caricature sketch artist

occupations.title: Floral Designers
occupations.description: Design, cut, and arrange live, dried, or artificial flowers and foliage
occupations.examples: Corsage maker, florist, flower arranger, event florist

occupations.title: Skincare Specialists
occupations.description: Provide skincare treatments to face and body to enhance an individual's appearance
occupations.examples: Facialist, electrologist, spa esthetician

occupations.title: Massage Therapists
occupations.description: Perform therapeutic massages of soft tissues and joints. May assist in the assessment of range of motion and muscle strength or propose client therapy plans
occupations.examples: Masseuse, deep tissue massage therapist, sports massage therapist

occupations.title: Barbers, Hairdressers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists
occupations.description: Provide beauty or barbering services, such as cutting, coloring, and styling hair, massaging and treating scalps, trimming beards or giving shaves
occupations.examples: Wig stylist, beautician, hair colorist, hair cutter

occupations.title: Shampooers
occupations.description: Shampoo and rinse customers' hair
occupations.examples: Scalp treatment specialist, shampoo assistant

occupations.title: Manicurists and Pedicurists
occupations.description: Clean and shape customers' fingernails and toenails. May polish or decorate nails
occupations.examples: Nail technician, fingernail sculptor, nail painter

occupations.title: Eyebrow and Eyelash Technicians
occupations.description: Enhance and maintain clients' eyebrows using techniques such as threading, waxing, or tweezing. Enhance clients' eyelashes using techniques such as tinting or applying extensions
occupations.examples: Eyebrow waxer

occupations.title: Makeup Artists
occupations.description: Design and apply makeup looks
occupations.examples: Wedding makeup artist, party makeup artist

occupations.title: Exercise Trainers and Group Fitness Instructors
occupations.description: Instruct or coach groups or individuals in exercise activities for the primary purpose of personal fitness. Demonstrate techniques and form, observe participants, and explain to them corrective measures necessary to improve their skills. Develop and implement individualized approaches to exercise
occupations.examples: Aerobics trainer, yoga instructor, personal trainer

occupations.title: Tattoo Artists and Piercers
occupations.description: Design and execute tattoos on a client's skin, often using a needle and ink. Create openings in the human body for the insertion of jewelry. May consult clients on aftercare to promote healing and prevent infection
occupations.examples: Tattoo artist, ear piercer, nose piercer

occupations.title: Tailors
occupations.description: Design, make, alter, repair, or fit garments
occupations.examples: Tailor, seamstress, clothing alterations worker

occupations.title: Shoe and Leather Workers and Repairers
occupations.description: Construct, decorate, or repair leather and leather-like products, such as luggage, shoes, and saddles. May use hand tools
occupations.examples: Cobbler, shoe shiner

occupations.title: Golf Caddies
occupations.description: Assist a golfer during a round of golf by providing practical support and strategic advice. May carry the golfer's bag, manage their clubs, offer guidance on club selection or course strategy
occupations.examples: Golf caddie, golf cart attendant

occupations.title: Self-Enrichment Teachers
occupations.description: Teach or instruct individuals or groups for the primary purpose of self-enrichment, rather than for an occupational objective, educational attainment, competition, or fitness
occupations.examples: Knitting instructor, piano teacher, art instructor, dance teacher

occupations.title: Recreational and Tour Pilots
occupations.description: Pilot and navigate the flight of fixed-wing aircraft, helicopters, or other airborne vehicle for recreational or touring purposes. Excludes regional, national, and international airline pilots, and emergency services pilots
occupations.examples: Helicopter tour pilot, hot air balloon aeronaut, skydiving pilot

occupations.title: Tour Guides
occupations.description: Guide individuals or groups on sightseeing tours or through places of interest, such as industrial establishments, public buildings, and art galleries
occupations.examples: Museum guide, sightseeing guide

occupations.title: Travel Guides
occupations.description: Plan, organize, and conduct long-distance travel, tours, and expeditions for individuals and groups (covering both indoor and outdoor locations)
occupations.examples: Cruise director, river expedition guide

occupations.title: Sports and Recreation Instructors
occupations.description: Teach or instruct individuals or groups for the primary purpose of recreation, rather than for an occupational objective, educational attainment, competition, or fitness
occupations.examples: Diving instructor, ski instructor, tennis teacher, surfing instructor

occupations.title: Parking and Valet Attendants
occupations.description: Park vehicles or issue tickets for customers in a parking lot or garage. May park or tend vehicles in environments such as a hotel or restaurant. May collect fee
occupations.examples: Parking garage attendant, valet parker

occupations.title: Taxi and Rideshare Drivers and Chauffeurs
occupations.description: Drive a motor vehicle to transport passengers on a planned or unplanned basis
occupations.examples: Cab driver, personal driver, platform/app-based rideshare driver

occupations.title: Shuttle Drivers
occupations.description: Drive a motor vehicle to transport passengers on a planned route and scheduled basis. May collect a fare. Excludes taxi and rideshare drivers, chauffeurs, municipal bus drivers, and school bus drivers
occupations.examples: Airport shuttle driver, hotel shuttle driver, rental car shuttle driver

occupations.title: Goods Delivery People
occupations.description: Drive truck or other vehicle to deliver goods, such as food products, appliances, or furniture, or pick up or deliver packages. May also take orders or collect payment at point of delivery
occupations.examples: Pizza delivery driver, grocery delivery driver, floral delivery, bicycle courier, package delivery person, appliance delivery driver, furniture delivery person, app/platform-based delivery person

occupations.title: Personal Vehicle and Equipment Cleaners
occupations.description: Wash or otherwise clean personal vehicles, machinery, and other equipment. Use such materials as water, cleaning agents, brushes, cloths, and hoses
occupations.examples: Car wash attendant, auto detailer, boat waxer

occupations.title: Private and Charter Bus Drivers
occupations.description: Drive bus or motor coach for charters or private carriage. May assist passengers with baggage
occupations.examples: Motor coach bus driver, tour bus driver

occupations.title: Water Taxi Operators and Charter Boat Workers
occupations.description: Operate water taxi boats or provide services to passengers on private charter boats. May assist in navigational activities
occupations.examples: Water taxi captain, air boat operator, charter boat deckhand, charter boat steward

occupations.title: Rickshaw, Pedicab, and Carriage Drivers
occupations.description: Operate rickshaw, pedicab, or carriage to transport passengers
occupations.examples: Horse drawn carriage driver, bike taxi driver

occupations.title: Home Movers
occupations.description: Manually move furniture, music instruments, art, antiques, boxes, luggage, or other materials to or from a home or dwelling
occupations.examples: Furniture mover, packer, piano mover, art mover

occupations.title: Gas Pump Attendant
occupations.description: Pump gas for customers at a gas station. May also clean the windshield, check the oil level, or check the tire pressure of the customer's car in conjunction with the car being refueled
occupations.examples: Gas pumper
